variant: give me the complete guide: table of products, weekly timeline, checklist, and key stats for starting skincare from zero

request.message: give me the complete guide: table of products, weekly timeline, checklist, and key stats for starting skincare from zero

response_body.response:
I've got thin evidence here—your docs focus on mistakes and barrier repair, not a full beginner protocol. I'll build this with what's in your module plus foundational skincare structure. --- **the core principle:** start with a clean barrier, one active at a time, SPF non-negotiable.

your skin is 23, likely still producing oil, so assume combo/normal unless you're already flaking or burning. **week 1–2: barrier repair phase.** cleanser + moisturizer + SPF only. no actives yet.

this lets you see your baseline and confirm your barrier isn't already compromised. **week 3+: introduce one active.** if acne-prone, start azelaic AM. if aging/texture, start a low-dose retinoid PM.

never stack acids + retinoids + vitamin c at once. **SPF every day.** non-negotiable. UV worsens pigmentation, breaks collagen, increases inflammation. ---     --- **what you're missing from the docs:** specific product brands, exact retinoid strength, and azelaic dosage. ask if you want me to pull those.

for now, the structure is: repair barrier first, introduce one active slowly, protect with SPF daily. that's 90% of the win.

products.name: The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.description: Azelaic acid for redness, PIH, and mild rosacea — pregnancy-safe.

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.

rows: once daily or twice if oily

steps.label: week 1–2: foundation
steps.detail: cleanser + moisturizer + SPF only. observe skin, confirm barrier health.  no actives.

steps.label: week 3: introduce active
steps.detail: add azelaic AM (acne) or retinoid PM (aging). one only.  watch for irritation.

steps.label: week 4–6: stabilize
steps.detail: keep routine consistent. skin adapts to active.  barrier strengthens with ceramides + panthenol.

steps.label: week 7–8: assess
steps.detail: evaluate results. if no irritation, can consider second active (not before week 8).  if irritated, pause and repair.

items: get a gentle cleanser (CeraVe, cetaphil, or similar)
get a moisturizer with ceramides and panthenol
week 3: add one active (azelaic or retinoid, not both)
check for barrier damage signs: redness, stinging, dryness, flaking
if irritated: pause actives, focus on ceramides + panthenol for 1–2 weeks
never stack acids + retinoids + vitamin c at the same time

cards.label: maximum at a time
cards.hint: stacking overwhelms skin and reduces absorption

cards.label: SPF compliance
cards.hint: UV worsens pigmentation, breaks collagen, increases inflammation

cards.label: time to visible change
cards.hint: depends on active and skin type
